title: Recurrent urinary symptoms without infection
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.
assessment.rationale: Supports adult male urinary-symptom, medicine, examination, diary and urinalysis context for recurrent urinary symptoms without infection; no decision is generated.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
source.population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
source.exclusions: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

assessment.text: Supports urgency-incontinence or overactive-bladder symptom context, relevant contributing factors, and clinician-performed pelvic-floor assessment in adult women.
assessment.rationale: Supports adult female incontinence, urgency and contributing-factor assessment for recurrent urinary symptoms without infection; no decision is generated.

source.title: Urinary incontinence and pelvic organ prolapse in women: management
source.population: Women aged 18 and over with urinary incontinence or overactive-bladder symptoms, with UTI, voiding, referral, and treatment qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial assessment, urine testing, bladder diaries, conservative management, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Partial for a sex-unspecified frequency or urgency workflow. Exact only for selected female urinary-incontinence or overactive-bladder assessment and clinician-documented conservative-management fields; medicines, procedures, fixed follow-up, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
